name: multi-search
description: Search for multiple patterns in files or directories in one pass, showing results grouped by pattern with per-pattern limits, context, exclude patterns, files-only mode, case-insensitive search, and per-file max-count.

parameters.name: pattern
parameters.description: Search pattern(s); use pattern:N syntax for per-pattern head limit (e.g. 'func.*foo:5' shows at most 5 matches)

parameters.name: target
parameters.description: File path or directory to search

parameters.name: --cd=DIR, -C DIR
parameters.description: Change to directory before searching (replaces cd + grep pattern)

parameters.name: -l, --files-with-matches
parameters.description: List only filenames with matches (union of all patterns)

parameters.name: -C, --context=N
parameters.description: Show N lines of context around matches for ALL patterns

parameters.name: -d, --max-depth=N
parameters.description: Max directory depth for recursive search

parameters.name: --head=N
parameters.description: Show at most N total output lines across all patterns

parameters.name: --per-pattern-head=N
parameters.description: Default per-pattern head limit for all patterns (can be overridden per pattern via pattern:N syntax)

parameters.name: --max-count=N
parameters.description: Stop after N matching lines per file (grep -m)

examples.expected: Groups of results for 3 patterns, each with respective head limits

cost_saving_rationale: Combines N separate grep + head calls into one step, saving N-1 steps; per-pattern limits avoid overfetching one pattern at the expense of others; grouped output eliminates the need for separate calls to explore different aspects of the same file; --max-count replaces grep -m usage for limiting results per file.
